task_completion.description: The Volume Rendering module is active and rendering is enabled
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Volume Rendering' module is visible in the side panel. The 'eye' icon next to the Volume field is toggled on. A 3D volume is visible in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Liver parenchyma is visible as a distinct anatomical structure
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large organ mass (the liver) is visible in the abdominal region of the 3D volume, typically rendered in reddish, brownish, or soft-tissue colors.

task_completion.description: Contrast-enhanced vascular structures are clearly visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: Brighter, high-intensity (often white or light yellow) branching structures representing the portal vasculature are visible within or on the surface of the liver mass.

task_completion.description: Transparency settings allow visualization of internal structures
task_completion.visual_evidence: The liver tissue appears semi-transparent or translucent rather than opaque, allowing the brighter internal vascular tree to be seen through the tissue.

task_completion.description: The 3D view is rotated to an anterior-oblique perspective
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view shows the liver from the front-side angle (anterior-oblique), optimizing the visibility of the liver volume and its internal vessels.

task_completion.description: The final screenshot is saved to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file browser or save dialog confirms the existence of 'liver_volume_rendering.png' in the folder '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Slicer GUI to configure rendering
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Slicer application window with panels (Volume Rendering), sliders, or buttons being manipulated, rather than purely terminal commands.

integrity.description: The rendering is generated within the Slicer application
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D visualization is displayed within the specific 3D viewport of the Slicer interface, surrounded by standard Slicer UI elements (slice views, toolbar).

integrity.description: The agent did not simply display a pre-existing image
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D view is active (e.g., orientation cube visible, background gradient typical of Slicer), not a static image opened in an image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is IRCADb Patient 5 (Contrast-Enhanced CT). The liver is a large organ in the upper right abdomen. Correct volume rendering requires a transfer function where soft tissue (40-80 HU) is semi-transparent (reddish/brown) and contrast-enhanced vessels (150-300 HU) are opaque/bright white. The view should reveal the branching vascular tree inside the liver.
